questions.text: When you are assigned a task that you have never done before, what do you usually do?
options: A. Avoid the task if possible
B. Wait until someone explains everything step-by-step
C. Research the task, ask questions if needed, and attempt it
D. Delay starting until someone else handles it

questions.text: Your manager gives you an urgent task close to the project deadline. What would you do?
options: A. Complain about the timing
B. Ignore the new task and continue your current work
C. Reprioritize your work and focus on completing the urgent task
D. Ask someone else to take responsibility

questions.text: The project requires you to occasionally work beyond normal hours. How do you respond?
options: A. Refuse to work after office hours
B. Agree but work with minimal effort
C. Support the team and work extra hours when necessary
D. Try to avoid such tasks

questions.text: Your team receives sudden changes in project requirements. What is your reaction?
options: A. Complain about the management decision
B. Continue with the old plan
C. Adapt quickly and adjust your work accordingly
D. Wait for someone else to solve the issue

questions.text: You are given multiple tasks with tight deadlines. How do you manage them?
options: A. Panic and delay work
B. Work randomly on whichever task appears first
C. Prioritize tasks based on urgency and importance
D. Leave some tasks incomplete

questions.text: If you make a mistake that affects your work, what would you do?
options: A. Hide the mistake
B. Blame someone else
C. Admit the mistake and try to fix it immediately
D. Ignore it and hope nobody notices

questions.text: A teammate has a different working style than yours. What do you do?
options: A. Avoid working with them
B. Force them to follow your method
C. Try to understand their style and collaborate effectively
D. Report them to the manager

questions.text: You are asked to learn a new tool or technology quickly for a project. What would you do?
options: A. Refuse because it is not your expertise
B. Wait for formal training before starting
C. Learn through tutorials, practice, and ask for guidance if needed
D. Complain about the requirement

questions.text: Your team is facing a major issue and no one is taking initiative. What would you do?
options: A. Stay silent and wait
B. Assume someone else will handle it
C. Suggest possible solutions and take initiative
D. Ignore the problem

questions.text: When you receive constructive criticism from your manager, how do you react?
options: A. Take it personally and feel upset
B. Ignore the feedback
C. Accept the feedback and improve your work
D. Argue to defend your actions

questions.text: A colleague asks for help while you are busy with your own work. What do you do?
options: A. Refuse immediately
B. Ignore their request
C. Help them if possible while managing your own tasks
D. Tell them to ask someone else

questions.text: When working under high pressure, how do you usually behave?
options: A. Get frustrated and stop working
B. Complain frequently to others
C. Stay calm and focus on solving the problem
D. Avoid responsibility

questions.text: If your manager suddenly assigns you work outside your job role, what would you do?
options: A. Refuse the task immediately
B. Do the task with minimal effort
C. Accept it as an opportunity to learn and contribute
D. Delay the task intentionally

questions.text: When joining a new team, how do you usually behave?
options: A. Avoid interaction with others
B. Wait for others to approach you
C. Introduce yourself and try to build professional relationships
D. Act superior to others

questions.text: If a project plan fails unexpectedly, what would you do?
options: A. Give up on the task
B. Blame external factors
C. Analyze the issue and try a new approach
D. Wait for someone else to fix it
